caveats: a: The official classification separates pension-fund portfolio management into NAICS 523920 rather than 525110.
a: Form 5500 work demonstrates administrative activity but does not create an external customer-and-revenue model for the fund itself.
a: The frozen labor-share input is missing and was not replaced or re-estimated.
rho: Mandatory electronic reporting is evidence of digital workflow, not of AI deployment or labor realization.
rho: Governance, fiduciary review, data security, and benefit accuracy would constrain implementation even if a valid operator population existed.
rho: No proxy can repair the missing lens denominator.

e: Multiemployer or pooled arrangements may serve multiple participating employers but still exist as benefit vehicles for members rather than fee-earning external operators.
e: The frozen LMM firm count is missing and was not replaced with plan counts, filings, or assets.

s5: The frozen firm-count denominator is missing.
s5: No public transaction series was found for the nonexistent screened population.

q: Portfolio-management and third-party-administration economics belong to other NAICS industries.
q: No receipts denominator exists for the frozen dataset input.

d5: Pension plan assets or participant counts cannot substitute for constant-price operator service quantity.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: The classified entity is a benefit fund rather than a fee-earning operator serving external customers. It receives or holds plan resources and pays retirement benefits for the sponsor's employees or members; separable portfolio management and administration are supplied by entities in other industries.
lens.excluded_activities: Sponsor- or member-only pension funds and plans, captive benefit arrangements, non-control financial vehicles, shells, portfolio managers classified in 523920, annuity underwriters classified in 524113, and third-party administrators classified outside this code.
lens.included_activities: No qualifying recurring outsourced-service activity was identified inside the official 525110 definition; the code consists of pension funds, plans, or programs providing retirement income benefits exclusively for a sponsor's employees or members.

caveats: The official classification separates pension-fund portfolio management into NAICS 523920 rather than 525110.
Form 5500 work demonstrates administrative activity but does not create an external customer-and-revenue model for the fund itself.
The frozen labor-share input is missing and was not replaced or re-estimated.
a.change_evidence: Evidence that fee-earning external pension-service operators are substantively classified in 525110, followed by payroll-by-occupation and task-time data for those operators separating recordkeeping, reporting, participant service, accounting, investment oversight, governance, and already-automated work, would be required to estimate a.
a.rationale: There is no eligible outsourced-service firm population in the official code to support a wage-weighted share of exposed tasks. Pension funds may perform or procure recordkeeping, reporting, participant communication, accounting, and investment oversight, but those activities cannot be converted into the lens's operator labor share, and the frozen compensation-to-receipts input is also unavailable.

caveats: Form 5500 reporting obligations demonstrate recurring plan administration but not lens-qualified market demand.
Pension plan assets or participant counts cannot substitute for constant-price operator service quantity.
Adjacent service-provider demand belongs to other industries.
d5.change_evidence: A defined eligible operator population plus a constant-price series of external pension-service contracts, administered participants, transactions, renewals, and fee revenue for entities actually classified in 525110 would be required to estimate d5.
d5.rationale: Retirement-benefit obligations and reporting create continuing activity, but there is no current external service basket supplied by eligible 525110 operators whose constant-price quantity can be compared over five years. Plan counts, assets, contributions, beneficiaries, and outsourced service demand are different quantities.

caveats: The estimate is a structural lens judgment from the industry definition, not a measured firm census.
Multiemployer or pooled arrangements may serve multiple participating employers but still exist as benefit vehicles for members rather than fee-earning external operators.
The frozen LMM firm count is missing and was not replaced with plan counts, filings, or assets.
e.change_evidence: A firm-level classification audit using Census Business Register records, Form 5500 service-provider schedules, legal-entity documents, customer contracts, fee revenue, control rights, and normalized EBITDA could identify any exceptional 525110 entities that actually serve external customers and materially change eligibility.
e.rationale: The Census definition places legal pension funds and plans serving only a sponsor's employees or members in 525110 and explicitly places pension portfolio managers in 523920. Under the fixed lens, the defined entities are captive or non-control financial vehicles rather than external recurring-service firms; a small high bound allows for real-world miscoding or an unusual independently controlled structure without redefining the industry.

l.detail: No receipts concept for this code (government / SUSB out of scope). QCEW government wages exist ($21,195,756) but there is no revenue denominator; labor_share left null.
l.rationale: dataset labor_share has no defensible value

n.detail: margin 0.2102 (margins.json[52]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Financial Svcs. (Non-bank & Insurance) EBITDA/Sales 21.02%. Note: Damodaran reports 0.00% for banks (EBITDA not meaningful); bank codes inherit this non-bank default and are flagged ESTIMATE like all n_band values.); revenue/employee $605,324 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 525); DATA GAP: no SUSB size-class data for this code; n_band could not be computed — the research run must supply it with a shown chain, quality ESTIMATE
n.rationale: dataset n_band has no defensible value

caveats: Persistent legal accountability does not establish an eligible outsourced operator within 525110.
Software may change fund administration without changing the classification mismatch.
The denominator is absent rather than economically zero.
o.change_evidence: A verified 525110 external-service basket and buyer-level evidence on software substitution, self-service, retained fiduciary accountability, vendor replacement, and contract cancellations would be required to estimate o.
o.rationale: There is no lens-qualified year-five service quantity to divide between accountable operators, self-service, and software. Fiduciary and reporting accountability may persist for pension plans, but the accountable entity is a benefit vehicle or its separately classified providers rather than an eligible operator of this code.

caveats: Fund expense savings are not equivalent to commercial benefit retention by an outsourced operator.
Portfolio-management and third-party-administration economics belong to other NAICS industries.
No receipts denominator exists for the frozen dataset input.
q.change_evidence: Evidence of eligible 525110 operating companies with external fee contracts, billing units, renewal and gainsharing terms, technology costs, and realized margins after automation would be required to estimate q.
q.rationale: The classified fund does not have a commercial pricing contract under which an operator retains an implemented gross benefit after customer sharing or repricing. Savings would accrue within the benefit arrangement or through contracts with service providers classified elsewhere, so q is undefined for the frozen lens.

caveats: Mandatory electronic reporting is evidence of digital workflow, not of AI deployment or labor realization.
Governance, fiduciary review, data security, and benefit accuracy would constrain implementation even if a valid operator population existed.
No proxy can repair the missing lens denominator.
rho.change_evidence: A verified population of fee-earning 525110 operators plus system-level deployment data on document processing, participant service, reconciliation, compliance review, error rates, human overrides, staffing, and realized labor release would be required to estimate rho.
rho.rationale: Without an eligible operator population or a defensible exposed labor quantity, there is no meaningful denominator for the share implemented within five years. Electronic reporting and data-rich administration indicate technical opportunities, but implementation by sponsor-controlled funds is not implementation by a screened outsourced-service operator.

caveats: Plan or fund events are not interchangeable with qualifying control transfers of operating firms.
The frozen firm-count denominator is missing.
No public transaction series was found for the nonexistent screened population.
s5.change_evidence: A verified list of eligible fee-earning 525110 firms and five years of completed control transactions involving those exact operating companies, excluding plan mergers, mandate changes, sponsor reorganizations, and benefit-vehicle terminations, would be required to estimate s5.
s5.rationale: A qualifying control-transfer probability cannot be defined because the eligible-firm denominator is absent. Sponsor changes, trustee changes, plan mergers, asset-manager mandates, terminations, and internal reorganizations are not necessarily acquisitions of a transferable outsourced-service operator.

principal_driver: Recurring digital reporting and participant administration indicate automation potential in the surrounding pension ecosystem.
principal_weakness: The code contains sponsor- or member-only benefit vehicles rather than external recurring-service firms, and both frozen dataset anchors are missing.

caveats: S1's registered title identifies it as a 2022 NAICS definition, but the cited Census archive page is explicitly labeled 1997 NAICS. Its displayed 525110 definition and 523920 cross-reference support the score-driving structural claim, so this is a source-vintage metadata defect rather than a material defect.

The e high bound of 0.02 is an acknowledged judgment without a measured firm census. Because e is labeled ESTIMATE and F remains MISSING due to absent n and s5, changing this bound would not change the absent base tier or EVIDENCE_FIRST action.

sources_audited.note: The Census page directly defines 525110 as legal pension funds, plans, or programs providing retirement income benefits exclusively for a sponsor's employees or members, and it cross-references pension-fund portfolio management to 523920. The registered title inaccurately calls this a 2022 definition; the reopened archive page is labeled 1997 NAICS.

sources.supports: Defines 525110 as legal pension funds, plans, or programs providing retirement income benefits exclusively for a sponsor's employees or members and classifies pension portfolio management in 523920.
sources.title: 2022 NAICS Sector 52 Definitions: Pension Funds

sources.supports: Describes the Form 5500 Series as the ERISA and Internal Revenue Code reporting and disclosure framework for employee benefit plans and requires electronic filing of covered annual reports and schedules.
sources.title: U.S. Department of Labor: Form 5500 Series

title: Pension Funds
